task_completion.description: Navigate to the local Artifactory instance
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to http://localhost:8082 showing the JFrog Artifactory login screen or dashboard.

task_completion.description: Log in using the provided admin credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory interface showing the user is authenticated as 'admin' (e.g., user profile icon or name in the top right corner).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Artifactory Trash Can
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory UI displaying the 'Trash Can' page or view, typically found in the Artifacts or Administration menu.

task_completion.description: Locate the deleted artifact in the Trash Can
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' visible in the list of items within the Trash Can.

task_completion.description: Initiate the restore action for the artifact
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu, button, or confirmation dialog showing the 'Restore' action being selected or executed for 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'.

task_completion.description: Verify the artifact is restored to the correct repository
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifacts Tree or repository browser showing 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' successfully located inside 'example-repo-local' (specifically under the path org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/).

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JFrog Artifactory web interface rather than using terminal curl commands or API scripts to restore the artifact.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual JFrog Artifactory application
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual elements match a genuine JFrog Artifactory web interface, not a text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent actually restored the file from the Trash Can rather than uploading a new copy
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen navigating the Trash Can interface and using the built-in restore functionality, rather than downloading the JAR from the internet and using the 'Deploy' button.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target artifact is 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'. It must be restored to the repository 'example-repo-local'. The exact path in the repository after restoration should be 'org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'. The Artifactory instance is hosted at http://localhost:8082 and accessed with user 'admin'.
